commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer security direction for the ACPX payload-authentication contract and Firecrawl SSRF boundary.; Needs attention: The diff attempts security hardening but leaves concrete ACPX payload-authentication and Firecrawl SSRF/compatibility concerns.; Review finding: [P1] Use ESM crypto in signed proxy mode — extensions/acpx/src/runtime-internals/mcp-proxy.mjs:23 - [P1] Make... (sha=18a4ae1536f7c466bce91bc3f5b685493614f7b0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ACPX proxy payload signature and command-prefix checks with new ACPX tests, and changes Firecrawl scrape validation to async DNS checks plus IP-rewritten scrape URLs.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path for the PR findings: signed ACPX mode executes a CommonJS require inside .mjs, and Firecrawl rewrites a normal HTTPS hostname to an IP before posting the scrape request. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer security direction for the ACPX payload-authentication contract and Firecrawl SSRF boundary.

target.title: security: harden ACPX proxy and Firecrawl SSRF protection
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACPX proxy had a potential command injection risk if the base64 `--payload` was manipulated. Firecrawl scrape validation used literal hostname checks, which are vulnerable to DNS rebinding (TOCTOU SSRF).
- Why it matters: These could lead to arbitrary code execution on the host (ACPX) or internal network enumeration/scanning (Firecrawl).
- What changed: 
  - ACPX: Added `assertSafeCommand` to `mcp-proxy.mjs` and support for `OPENCLAW_MCP_PROXY_ALLOWED_COMMAND_PREFIX`.
  - Firecrawl: Upgraded `assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed` to be async and use `resolvePinnedHostnameWithPolicy` for robust DNS-aware SSRF checks.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The browser extension's use of eval (Code Mode) was not touched in this PR as it's an intended feature, though noted as a risk.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to security audit.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: DNS-rebinding SSRF in Firecrawl and Command Injection in ACPX Proxy.
- Real environment tested: Local development environment (win32).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Updated and ran `extensions/firecrawl/src/firecrawl-tools.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix:
  The `assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed` now correctly fails for targets like `http://127.0.0.1` and `http://169.254.169.254` even if they were previously bypassable via DNS rebinding because we now pin the DNS resolution result.
- Observed result after fix: Tests pass with async validation.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/firecrawl/src/firecrawl-tools.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Blocking private/internal IPs during scrape target validation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It directly exercises the validation logic used before any network fetch.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`) - DNS resolution added to SSRF check.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`) - Hardened ACPX proxy.
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Risk of SSRF/Command injection reduced by enforcing centralized policies.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Firecrawl blocks internal IPs; ACPX proxy has prefix support.
- Edge cases checked: Valid public URLs still allowed.

## REVIEW NOTE FOR MAINTAINERS
The Firecrawl check is now asynchronous to allow for DNS resolution. All internal call sites within the extension have been updated to `await` this check.
target.author: thesomewhatyou
